I find the Japanese culture very different than the Chinese culture. We enjoy yum cha whereas they enjoy drinking green tea at bars, soaking up the atmosphere rather than at home. My Japanese friend Agnes is a Taurus while her boyfriend is an Aquarius. She's faithful to him and would never cheat on him. When she broke up with her ex, she was driven over the edge. Even when a stranger talked to her, there was an edge of hostility to her voice. But as soon as she had a new relationship target, it took the edge of her emotional upset. Her ex is a swimming champion who far outshines us in any swimming styles. He's talk and lean who definitely has the edge over a lot of swimmers. Recently, her boyfriend was in scuffles with a guy who was hitting on Agnes. The police showed up later and jealousy as assigned as the morive for the scuffles. Last Tuesday, they dined in a restaurant in a hotel which is midway between the supermarket and her place. They lined for almost an hour outside the restaurant. The line was so big that it stretched from the counter through the lobby area and out the main entrance of the hotel. A stout guy tried to push in at the head of the queue but was caught. Last Thursday, Agnes and I met at a midway point between her place and mine then she took me to the shuttle bus bound for her place. A group of people was in the way so she said "Excuse me." The line was so huge, stretching down the road to the furthest minibus stop. We stood in line for half an hour until three shuttle buses came along at once. In the meantime, some people mistook this long line for the line got other places. A couple came up and asked "Are you in the line for The Harbourside?" as they wanna be sure that it's the right line. A burly man cut in line midway through the line. Later, a slim guy jumped the line. The svelte woman behind him said "Excuse me. The end of the line is back there." A petite lady stood beside a slender man who was midway through the line. While pretending to look at her phone, she edged furtively beside him who didn't realize she was there. Some people might have noticed it but ignored it. Agnes seemed on edge. It turns out she has a job interview next Friday. Never has she felt so on edge before an interview. Agnes' elder sister is currently a head chef at a five-star hotel and feels she's found her niche. Her elder brother has also found his niche as a professor, having worked as a resesrcher for three and a half years. He sometimes has a doze at lunchtime because he has do much work to do that he feels drained. He usually naps on the sofa by the fireplace or at his desk when everybody else's eating lunch outside. He once brought his work along with him to the library where he dozed off at the table. Having a snooze especially by the fire is a marvelous way to recharge your energy though. Agnes' younger sister has just completed her PhD at Peking University and is on the way to becoming a professor. China is on the way to becoming the world's strongest economy. Her younger brother is healthy and robust. He does a lot of strength and conditioning exercises in his robust sneakers. After his gym sessions, he usually drinks a cup of robust coffee and a glass of robust wine. He lives in Japan where the economy and the political system are robust. Her eldest sister has opened an antique shop which created a great impression on me  Theres an exquisite antique which engages my interest. I bet the store engages lots of passers-by. I had a favorable impression of the store. Agnes' auntie has recently passed away at the age of ninety eight. I offer my sincere condolences to his auntie's family. Her friend Leonardo said "My condolences on the loss of your auntie." Her mom also sent her condolences to her family. Her dad wrote "Our condolences go to her family." Agnes hadn't heard from her cousin for years until last Thursday when she received a call from him. They chatted with exquisite delight.

replies.msg:
紅燈行、綠燈停

當時，紅衛兵們認定紅色是革命的象徵，所以在1966年發佈「通令」，要求交通信號燈改為「紅燈行、綠燈停」，因為要「在紅燈的照耀下前進」。

這樣的舉措一度造成了北京市內交通的嚴重混亂，後來在周恩來總理的努力下才撤銷「通令」，恢復常態。

不過北京的雖然取消了，其他地方由於鞭長莫及，「紅燈行，綠燈停」卻持續了很久…

靠左行

和認定紅色是革命象徵一樣，「左」也被紅衛兵們看做是革命的象徵，「右」則是走資派。於是他們要求車輛行駛時，由靠右行駛改成靠左行駛。想想也是醉了。
